name: West Virginia SNAP Benefits
description: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program providing monthly food benefits via EBT card to eligible low-income West Virginia households through the DHHR.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal income and resource guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 130% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: West Virginia Medicaid
description: State Medicaid program providing health coverage for low-income West Virginians including managed care through Mountain Health Trust for most enrollees.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income children, pregnant women, adults up to 138% FPL, elderly, and disabled individuals
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL; children up to 300% FPL; pregnant women up to 185% FPL

name: WorkForce West Virginia - Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial benefits to West Virginia workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own while they search for new employment.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own and meet wage requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period

name: West Virginia Housing Development Fund
description: State housing finance agency providing affordable mortgage programs, down payment assistance, rental programs, and housing development financing across West Virginia.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally up to 80% area median income

name: Legal Aid of West Virginia
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income West Virginians statewide in areas including housing, family law, consumer protection, public benefits, and elder law.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income West Virginia residents with civil legal needs
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125% of federal poverty level

name: WorkForce West Virginia - Career Centers
description: State workforce development centers providing job search assistance, resume help, career counseling, and training programs at American Job Centers across the state.
eligibilitySummary: Any West Virginia resident seeking employment or career development services

name: West Virginia LIHEAP - Energy Assistance
description: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program helping eligible West Virginia households pay heating and cooling bills through the DHHR.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households needing help with energy costs
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 150% of federal poverty level

name: West Virginia 211
description: Free statewide information and referral service connecting West Virginians with local health and human services resources around the clock.
eligibilitySummary: Any West Virginia resident seeking help or information about available services

name: Mountaineer Food Bank
description: Feeding America affiliate and the largest food bank in West Virginia distributing food to partner agencies across 48 counties serving hundreds of thousands of residents.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance across West Virginia

name: Facing Hunger Foodbank
description: Feeding America affiliate serving 17 counties in western West Virginia and southeastern Ohio with food distribution through partner pantries and programs.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance in western West Virginia

name: West Virginia Department of Veterans Assistance
description: Assists West Virginia veterans and their families with benefits claims, healthcare referrals, education, and access to the state veterans nursing facility.
eligibilitySummary: West Virginia veterans and their families

name: West Virginia Crisis Hotline - HELP4WV
description: Statewide crisis line providing support for mental health crises, substance use disorders, and emotional distress with referrals to treatment and recovery services.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in West Virginia experiencing a mental health or substance abuse crisis

name: West Virginia Child Care Assistance
description: Subsidized childcare program helping low-income working West Virginia families afford quality childcare for their children through the DHHR.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families with children needing childcare
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 150% of federal poverty level

name: West Virginia WIC Program
description: Women, Infants, and Children nutrition program providing supplemental foods, nutrition education, and health referrals for eligible women and children.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, breastfeeding, and postpartum women; infants and children under 5
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of federal poverty level

name: West Virginia TANF / WV Works
description: Temporary cash assistance program providing benefits and work-related support to very low-income families with dependent children to achieve self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families with dependent children
incomeLimitNotes: Income must be below WV Works payment standard for family size

name: Kanawha Valley Regional Transportation Authority (KRT) - Charleston
description: Public bus system serving the Charleston metropolitan area and Kanawha Valley with fixed routes and demand-response transit services.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in the Charleston/Kanawha Valley area; reduced fare for seniors and disabled riders

name: West Virginia Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free home energy efficiency improvements to low-income West Virginia households to reduce utility costs and improve comfort and safety.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: West Virginia Adult Education
description: Free adult education programs including GED preparation, basic literacy, English language classes, and workforce readiness training across the state.
eligibilitySummary: Adults age 16 and older not enrolled in K-12 school

name: West Virginia Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, and shelter referrals for survivors of domestic violence throughout West Virginia.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or at risk of domestic violence in West Virginia

name: Huntington Housing Authority
description: Provides public housing and Section 8 vouchers to low-income families, elderly, and disabled residents in the Huntington area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in the Huntington area
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 80% area median income

name: Charleston Housing Authority
description: Provides public housing and Housing Choice Vouchers to low-income families, elderly, and disabled residents in the Charleston area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in the Charleston area
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 80% area median income

name: West Virginia Division of Rehabilitation Services
description: Provides vocational rehabilitation services for West Virginians with disabilities including counseling, training, job placement, and assistive technology.
eligibilitySummary: West Virginia residents with physical or mental disabilities that impact employment

name: West Virginia CHIP (Children's Health Insurance Program)
description: Provides health coverage for uninsured children in West Virginia families that earn too much for Medicaid but cannot afford private insurance.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 in families with income too high for Medicaid
incomeLimitNotes: Family income up to 300% of federal poverty level

name: West Virginia Health Insurance Marketplace
description: Federal health insurance marketplace where West Virginians can compare and enroll in qualified health plans with potential premium tax credits.
eligibilitySummary: West Virginians without employer coverage or other qualifying health insurance
incomeLimitNotes: Premium tax credits available for households between 100-400% FPL

name: West Virginia Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: Provides discounted phone and broadband service to eligible low-income West Virginians to ensure access to essential telecommunications.
eligibilitySummary: Households participating in SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, or with income at or below 135% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 135% FPL or participation in qualifying federal program

name: West Virginia PROMISE Scholarship
description: Merit-based state scholarship covering tuition and fees at West Virginia public colleges for academically high-achieving students.
eligibilitySummary: West Virginia high school graduates with minimum ACT/SAT scores and GPA requirements
incomeLimitNotes: No income limit; merit-based

name: West Virginia WV Invests Grant
description: Last-dollar grant covering tuition at West Virginia community and technical colleges for adults pursuing certificates or associate degrees in high-demand fields.
eligibilitySummary: West Virginia residents pursuing eligible programs at community and technical colleges
incomeLimitNotes: No income limit; last-dollar after other financial aid

name: West Virginia Bureau of Senior Services
description: Provides services for seniors including Meals on Wheels, homemaker services, transportation, legal assistance, and caregiver support across the state.
eligibilitySummary: West Virginia residents age 60 and older and their caregivers
incomeLimitNotes: Some services have income requirements; others available to all seniors

name: Tri-State Transit Authority (TTA) - Huntington
description: Public bus system serving the Huntington tri-state area including parts of West Virginia, Kentucky, and Ohio with fixed routes.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in the Huntington tri-state area; reduced fare for seniors and disabled riders

name: West Virginia Community Action Partnership
description: Network of community action agencies across West Virginia providing emergency assistance, weatherization, Head Start, and self-sufficiency programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income West Virginia residents seeking emergency assistance and support services
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally at or below 200% FPL

name: Cabin Creek Health Systems
description: Federally qualified health center serving rural southern West Virginia with affordable primary care, dental, behavioral health, and pharmacy services.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in the service area; sliding fee scale for uninsured patients
incomeLimitNotes: Sliding fee scale based on income and family size
